mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion periods, or the predictive relationship reverses direction (Rank IC < -0.02) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening economic activity and higher wage income, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC = 0.00) across multiple rolling windows, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of months with payroll gains.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 0.4% monthly threshold, it forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (KS test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across macro and micro levels, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle phases signal supply-side exhaustion and impending recession, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns in late-cycle periods, or the signal produces positive returns in over 55% of triggered instances.
